name: Black Willow
summary: The fastest riparian pioneer for active stream banks and saturated soils. Salix is Tallamy keystone genus #3 — ~450 Lepidoptera species including Mourning Cloak, Viceroy, and Eastern Tiger Swallowtail. Earliest-blooming nectar of the year for emerging native bees.
notes: Native throughout PA. Brittle wood (don't plant near structures or driveways). Short-lived for a tree (60–80 years) but reproduces vegetatively and from seed continuously. Trivially propagates from live stakes — cut dormant 1-inch stems, push into wet ground in late winter, walk away. Free from any donor stand.

planting.aspect: Active stream banks; saturated wet hollows; eroding edges
planting.soil: Any wet soil; tolerates clay and organic; pH adaptable
planting.site_notes: Plant from live stakes — cut 18–24 inch sections of 1-inch-thick dormant wood in late winter, push 12 inches into wet ground, water once. No nursery purchase needed. Don't plant near structures (brittle). No deer protection needed.
